source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
title: Ensure Image Vulnerability Scanning is enabled

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
input_row.title: Ensure Image Vulnerability Scanning is enabled
input_row.description: Note: GCR is now deprecated, being superseded by Artifact Registry starting 15th May 2024. Runtime Vulnerability scanning is available via GKE Security Posture Scan images stored in Google Container Registry (GCR) or Artifact Registry (AR) for vulnerabilities.
input_row.rationale: Vulnerabilities in software packages can be exploited by malicious users to obtain unauthorized access to local cloud resources. GCR Container Analysis API or Artifact Registry Container Scanning API allow images stored in GCR or AR respectively to be scanned for known vulnerabilities. Impact: None.
input_row.audit: For Images Hosted in GCR: Using Google Cloud Console:  1. Go to GCR by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/gcr 2. Select Settings and check if Vulnerability scanning is Enabled. Using Command Line:  gcloud services list --enabled Ensure that the Container Registry API and Container Analysis API are listed in the output. For Images Hosted in AR: Using Google Cloud Console:  1. Go to AR by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/artifacts 2. Select Settings and check if Vulnerability scanning is Enabled. Using Command Line:  gcloud services list --enabled Ensure that Container Scanning API and Artifact Registry API are listed in the output.
input_row.remediation: For Images Hosted in GCR: Using Google Cloud Console  1. Go to GCR by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/gcr 2. Select Settings and, under the Vulnerability Scanning heading, click the TURN ON button. Using Command Line  gcloud services enable containeranalysis.googleapis.com For Images Hosted in AR: Using Google Cloud Console  1. Go to GCR by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/artifacts 2. Select Settings and, under the Vulnerability Scanning heading, click the ENABLE button. Using Command Line  gcloud services enable containerscanning.googleapis.com Default Value: By default, GCR Container Analysis and AR Container Scanning are disabled. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/analysis 2. https://cloud.google.com/artifact-analysis/docs/os-overview 3. https://console.cloud.google.com/marketplace/product/google/containerregistry.g oogleapis.com 4. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/about-configuration- scanning 5. https://containersecurity.googleapis.com

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control "Ensure Image Vulnerability Scanning is enabled" for images stored in Google Container Registry (GCR) or Artifact Registry (AR) can be fully verified via GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands. This assessment is driven by the ability to check the status of the necessary APIs (Container Analysis API and Container Scanning API) using command-line tools, as indicated in the audit field of the input control. This involves checking the enabled status of these APIs programmatically, which is doable using GCP's command-line interface.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_artifact_registry_vulnerability_scanning_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP APIs/SDK Methods:** Use Google Cloud SDK `gcloud` to interact with GCP services.
- **GCP CLI Commands:**
  - Use `gcloud services list --enabled` to list all enabled services, specifically checking for:
    - `containeranalysis.googleapis.com` for GCR
    - `containerscanning.googleapis.com` for AR
- **Resources to Enumerate:** Check all GCP projects where GCR or AR might be used.
- **Configuration Properties to Validate:** Ensure that Container Analysis API (for GCR) and Container Scanning API (for AR) are enabled.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:** 
  - **Pass:** If both `containeranalysis.googleapis.com` (for GCR) and `containerscanning.googleapis.com` (for AR) are listed among the enabled services.
  - **Fail:** If either or both APIs are not listed as enabled.
- **Example Command Execution:** 
  - Execute `gcloud services list --enabled` and confirm that the necessary APIs appear in the output list.

By confirming the enablement of these APIs, we verify that image vulnerability scanning is properly configured according to the GCP CIS Benchmark recommendations.
